summary: The trachea is approximately 10 cm long, extending from C6 to T4/T5, where it bifurcates at the carina.
The trachea is approximately 20 cm long, extending from C4 to T6.
The trachea extends from the larynx at C3 to T2 where it bifurcates.
The right main bronchus is wider, shorter, and more vertical than the left, making it the common site for foreign body aspiration.
The left main bronchus is wider and more vertical, making it the common site for foreign body aspiration.
Both main bronchi are identical in angle and diameter.
The right lung has three lobes and 10 bronchopulmonary segments; the left lung has two lobes.
The right lung has two lobes and the left has three lobes.
Both lungs have three lobes and 10 segments each.
The trachea has 16-20 C-shaped cartilage rings open posteriorly, bridged by the trachealis muscle.
The trachea has complete O-shaped cartilage rings along its entire length.
The trachea has 6-8 C-shaped cartilage rings open anteriorly.
For chest drainage, needle insertion should be over the upper border of the lower rib to avoid the neurovascular bundle.
Needle insertion should be over the lower border of the upper rib for chest drainage.
The intercostal neurovascular bundle runs along the upper border of each rib.
The lingula of the left lung is the anatomical equivalent of the middle lobe of the right lung.
The lingula is part of the upper lobe of the right lung.
The lingula is a separate lobe of the left lung.
